rqs: What are the time and space complexities of Quick, Merge, Heap, Radix, and Bucket Sort compared with Insertion, Selection, Bubble, Shell, and Tim Sort in academic journals and conferences?
Which of the advanced sorts (Quick, Merge, Heap, Radix, Bucket) provides better stability than the classic sorts (Insertion, Selection, Bubble, Shell, Tim) in open‑source repositories?
In which contexts (benchmark suites, industrial case studies) do advanced sorting algorithms outperform classic ones in terms of comparisons and swaps?
